message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker timestamps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -840,7 +840,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3mldb","title":"Implement real policy re-evaluation in synthesize_mitigation instead of signum*-5 heuristic (ops/mitigation_synthesis.rs:282)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:31.223543494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:21:12.120066911Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:21:12.120042426Z","close_reason":"Replaced signum*-5 with proportional adjustment: net_shift from policy diff values, clamped to [-100,100]. Rationale now includes policy_shift and adj.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mldb","depends_on_id":"bd-2oy0o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.574870740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":342,"issue_id":"bd-3mldb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FILE: crates/franken-node/src/ops/mitigation_synthesis.rs around lines 282-290
FUNCTION: synthesize_mitigation()

CURRENT CODE:
  fn synthesize_mitigation(baseline: &[Decision], diff: &str) -> Vec<MitigationAction> {
      // In a real system this would re-evaluate each event under the new policy.
      // Simplified: parse numeric changes from diff, produce inverse actions.
      let delta: i64 = diff.split_whitespace()
          .filter_map(|w| w.parse::<i64>().ok())
          .sum();
      vec![MitigationAction {
          adjustment: delta.signum() * -5,
          rationale: format!("auto-mitigation for delta {delta}"),
      }]
  }

PROBLEM: The mitigation synthesis uses a crude heuristic: sum any integers found in the diff string, take signum, multiply by -5. This has no relationship to actual policy evaluation. It produces meaningless mitigations that could make things worse. The function is supposed to re-evaluate each event under the proposed policy and synthesize actions that represent the difference.

REQUIRED FIX: Implement real mitigation synthesis:
  1. Parse the diff to understand what policy changes are proposed
  2. For each baseline decision, re-evaluate under the new policy
  3. Identify decisions that would change
  4. For each changed decision, produce a MitigationAction that describes: what changed, why, and what corrective action to take
  5. Include confidence scores and rationale based on the actual policy delta

CONSIDERATIONS:
- This is the capstone of the mitigation_synthesis chain — depends on both bd-1sonf (events) and bd-2oy0o (replay_baseline)
- The diff format needs to be well-defined — is it a structured policy diff or freeform text?
- MitigationAction.adjustment field seems numeric — may need to be richer (enum of action types?)
- The rationale should explain the policy-level reasoning, not just "auto-mitigation for delta N"
- Consider edge cases: empty diff, no changed decisions, all decisions changed
- This feeds into counterfactual simulation (tools/counterfactual_replay.rs) — check that interface

DEPENDS ON: bd-2oy0o (replay_baseline provides the baseline decisions to compare against)
","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3msjy","title":"Fail closed when cancel injection gate coverage is incomplete","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs` promises `INV-CIG-ALL-WORKFLOWS` and `INV-CIG-FULL-MATRIX`, but `run_full_gate()` currently iterates only whatever happens to be in `control_workflows` and treats missing/zero-point required workflows as zero cases instead of a hard failure. That lets the gate return PASS with only a partial control-workflow set registered.

What this bug must do:
1. Make the gate fail closed when any required `ControlWorkflow` is missing from the registered control workflow set.
2. Make the gate fail closed when a required workflow has no registered await points in the canonical framework.
3. Preserve PASS behavior for the fully registered default matrix.
4. Add focused regressions proving partial registration and zero-point coverage no longer pass.
5. Run focused Rust validation through `rch` only.

Acceptance criteria:
1. A gate with only a subset of required control workflows cannot return PASS.
2. Missing/zero-point workflow coverage is reflected as an explicit failure in the report.
3. The existing default control workflow registration still yields PASS with 21 control points.
4. Focused `rch` validation passes or the remote blocker is documented precisely.","notes":"Fresh-eyes follow-up source fixes landed in cancel_injection_gate.rs: fail closed on non-canonical await-point count mismatches and on canonical-label drift, with focused regressions for both. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 --check and git diff --check are clean. Remote validation remains blocked by repeated rch stalls after frankenengine-engine: builds 29750734287276257 and 29750734287276260 both hung in execute/remote_exec_start and were cancelled cleanly.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveGlen","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:56:30.520761076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:20:27.131578946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":224,"issue_id":"bd-3msjy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started fix in crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs. Root cause: run_full_gate() can return PASS with partial required workflow coverage because missing/zero-point workflows are treated as zero cases instead of explicit failures.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:56:44Z"},{"id":225,"issue_id":"bd-3msjy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented fail-closed coverage checks in crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs. run_full_gate() now iterates the required ControlWorkflow set, emits explicit failures when a required workflow is missing or has zero registered await points, and uses real await-point labels in failure details. Added regressions for partial required-workflow registration and zero-point required workflow coverage. rustfmt --check on the file passes. Focused rch run: build 29750734287276150 on vmi1156319 using cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib cancel_injection_gate -- --nocapture; progressed through dependency compile and into frankenengine-engine, then stalled in execute/remote_exec_start with last_progress_at 2026-03-22T04:00:18Z / detector_progress_stale=true, so I cancelled it cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-22T04:02:17Z"},{"id":226,"issue_id":"bd-3msjy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes review found and fixed a follow-up bug in my earlier cancel_injection_gate patch: synthetic missing-workflow and zero-await-point failures were reported with points_failed=1 but total_points=0, which made the per-workflow report internally inconsistent. run_full_gate now counts those structural failures as one synthetic workflow_registration point in both the per-workflow row and aggregate total_injection_points, and the regressions now assert points_passed + points_failed == total_points for those cases. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs passes. Follow-up remote validation used rch exec with target dir .rch-target-bd-3msjy-followup running cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib zero_point_required_workflow_fails_closed -- --exact --nocapture; build 29750734287276159 on vmi1153651 progressed through compile into frankenengine-engine, then stalled in execute/remote_exec_start with last_progress_at=2026-03-22T04:16:18.126007338+00:00 and detector_progress_stale=true, and was cancelled cleanly via rch cancel.","created_at":"2026-03-22T04:20:03Z"},{"id":227,"issue_id":"bd-3msjy","author":"OliveGlen","text":"Closure-quality follow-up fix in crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs: malformed await-point metadata already failed the gate closed, but the report only counted the malformed slots themselves. That under-reported total_points and omitted otherwise-valid registered points from the per-workflow matrix, which violated INV-CIG-FULL-MATRIX / INV-CIG-REPORT-COMPLETE whenever a workflow was only partially malformed. I changed malformed_await_point_failures() to emit a PointFailure for every registered await point once any metadata corruption is detected, and run_full_gate() now counts the full registered workflow width as failed on that path. Updated duplicate_or_holey_await_point_indices_fail_closed and added single_malformed_await_point_aborts_entire_workflow_report to prove partially malformed workflows no longer undercount coverage. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 --check and git diff --check are clean. Focused rch build 29750734287276245 (cargo test -p frankenengine-node single_malformed_await_point_aborts_entire_workflow_report -- --exact --nocapture) progressed through dependency compile into frankenengine-engine, then went progress-stale for 91s in execute/remote_exec_start on worker vmi1153651 and was cancelled locally.","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:06:48Z"},{"id":228,"issue_id":"bd-3msjy","author":"OliveGlen","text":"Fresh-eyes review found and fixed two more canonical-matrix gaps in crates/franken-node/src/connector/cancel_injection_gate.rs. First, required workflows with a shortened but otherwise canonical-looking await-point list could still undercount the report width, so run_full_gate() now fails closed on non-canonical point-count mismatches using point_count_mismatch_failures() and the regression non_canonical_await_point_count_fails_closed. Second, same-length same-index registrations with renamed await-point labels could still pass structural validation, so malformed_await_point_failures() now also validates canonical labels and renamed_await_point_label_fails_closed proves the gate rejects label drift. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 --check and git diff --check are clean. Focused rch validation build 29750734287276257 (non_canonical_await_point_count_fails_closed) progressed into frankenengine-engine and then stalled in execute/remote_exec_start with detector_progress_stale=true, so I cancelled it cleanly. Replacement focused build 29750734287276260 (renamed_await_point_label_fails_closed) reached the same frankenengine-engine boundary, then went progress-stale in execute/remote_exec_start after last_progress_at=2026-03-22T23:17:36.449533349+00:00, and was cancelled cleanly as well.","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:20:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3n2u","title":"[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Normative schema files and golden vectors are versioned and release-published; verification CLI passes full vector suite; vector changes require explicit changelog entry.

Expected Artifacts:
- `spec/FNODE_TRUST_SCHEMA_V1.cddl`, `vectors/fnode_trust_vectors_v1.json`, `artifacts/10.13/vector_verification_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3n2u/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3n2u/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Publish formal schema spec files and golden vectors for serialization, signatures, and control-channel frames." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:55.059574888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T14:55:48.209691291Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T14:55:48.209665232Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n2u","depends_on_id":"bd-29ct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3n3c3","title":"Investigate repeated rch quiet-phase stalls while compiling frankenengine-engine during franken_node bead revalidation","description":"Why this exists:
Multiple stale `franken_node` validation/closeout beads are now reproducing the same fresh blocker during `rch`-offloaded Rust validation. The source fixes appear present in-tree, sync succeeds, dependency compilation succeeds, and then the worker enters a long quiet phase while compiling sibling `frankenengine-engine` without producing a `franken_node` compiler/test verdict.

Fresh evidence captured on 2026-03-25:
- `bd-13d6r`: `cargo test -p frankenengine-node --features extended-surfaces --lib operator_routes -- --nocapture` reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0` on worker `vmi1153651` and then stayed silent for about two minutes until cancellation.
- `bd-2474c`: `cargo test -p frankenengine-node save_checkpoint_rejects_epoch_regression -- --exact --nocapture` reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0` on worker `vmi1149989` and then stayed silent for about one minute until cancellation.
- `bd-2r9uf` (from peer coordination): fresh `cargo check -p frankenengine-node --features extended-surfaces --lib` passed, but targeted offloaded test build entered `execute/remote_exec_start` and then made no progress for ~303s before cancellation.

What this bug must do:
1. Determine whether the quiet phase is an expected long compile, a remote-exec output/heartbeat gap, or a real stall/deadlock in sibling `frankenengine-engine` compilation.
2. Identify whether the failure belongs in `rch`, worker provisioning, `franken_engine`, or the way `franken_node` offloaded test commands are configured.
3. Produce one concrete unblock path for stale bead closeout (for example: a different worker recipe, target-dir strategy, narrower command, or explicit upstream fix).
4. Link the result back to affected stale beads so we stop re-discovering the same blocker piecemeal.

Acceptance criteria:
1. At least one affected command is reproduced with enough instrumentation to explain the quiet phase.
2. The owning layer (`rch`/worker, sibling `franken_engine`, or command/config shape) is identified with evidence.
3. A concrete workaround or follow-up owner is recorded.
4. At least one stale bead (`bd-13d6r`, `bd-2474c`, `bd-2r9uf`, `bd-3vdvh`) is either unblocked or explicitly linked to the shared blocker instead of carrying a generic stale note.","notes":"Fresh 2026-03-25 diagnostics after reproducing the quiet-phase on `bd-13d6r` and `bd-2474c`:

Observed stale-bead pattern:
- `bd-13d6r`: offloaded `cargo test -p frankenengine-node --features extended-surfaces --lib operator_routes -- --nocapture` reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0` on worker `vmi1153651`, then stayed silent for about two minutes before local cancellation.
- `bd-2474c`: offloaded `cargo test -p frankenengine-node save_checkpoint_rejects_epoch_regression -- --exact --nocapture` reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0` on worker `vmi1149989`, then stayed silent for about one minute before local cancellation.
- peer note on `bd-2r9uf`: fresh `cargo check -p frankenengine-node --features extended-surfaces --lib` passed, but targeted test build entered `execute/remote_exec_start` and then made no progress for ~303s before cancellation.

Low-cost `rch` diagnostics gathered before burning more cargo slots:
- `rch status` reported posture `remote-ready`, 7/7 healthy workers, 30/46 free slots, but also emitted warning alerts that circuits were opened for every worker involved in the recent stale-bead runs: `vmi1227854`, `vmi1293453`, `vmi1264463`, `vmi1153651`, `vmi1167313`, `vmi1156319`, `vmi1149989`.
- `rch queue` showed multiple concurrent active `franken_engine` builds on the same worker pool while the stale `franken_node` validation lanes were attempting to run.
- `rch doctor` passed all checks, which suggests the issue is not basic local client misconfiguration.

Current interpretation:
The repeated quiet phase is more likely a worker-fleet / remote-exec / sibling-engine compile contention issue than a fresh `franken_node` source regression. The next useful step should instrument or isolate the quiet phase itself rather than re-running more source-complete stale beads blindly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:00:45.086947155Z","created_by":"SilentFinch","updated_at":"2026-03-25T20:01:44.084317558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["franken-engine","infrastructure","rch","validation"]}

Current interpretation:
The repeated quiet phase is more likely a worker-fleet / remote-exec / sibling-engine compile contention issue than a fresh `franken_node` source regression. The next useful step should instrument or isolate the quiet phase itself rather than re-running more source-complete stale beads blindly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:00:45.086947155Z","created_by":"SilentFinch","updated_at":"2026-03-25T20:27:42.266696846Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["franken-engine","infrastructure","rch","validation"],"comments":[{"id":357,"issue_id":"bd-3n3c3","author":"AzureForest","text":"AzureForest: high-signal diagnostics posted in Agent Mail thread `bd-3n3c3`; current read is RCH stuck-detector / progress-visibility gap during long frankenengine-engine compiles, not a fresh franken_node source regression.","created_at":"2026-03-25T20:27:42Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3n58","title":"[10.13] Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Interface hash uses domain-separated derivation; invalid hashes block admission; telemetry exposes rejection code distribution.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/interface_hash.rs`, `tests/conformance/interface_hash_verification.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/interface_hash_rejection_metrics.csv`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3n58/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-3n58/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:

- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Add domain-separated interface-hash verification and admission failure telemetry." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:52.535107430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:35:56.503105126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:35:56.503078356Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n58","depends_on_id":"bd-17mb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nbsh","title":"Seal enterprise-governance report hash over full category compliance surface","description":"Why this exists: crates/franken-node/src/tools/enterprise_governance.rs::ComplianceReport exposes full per-category compliance breakdowns in categories, but generate_report() currently derives content_hash from only schema_version, coarse rule/assessment counts, gate action, category count, and blocked_rules. Materially different category compliance distributions can therefore alias to the same content_hash when those coarse values stay constant. What this task must do: bind ComplianceReport.content_hash to the full semantically relevant category compliance surface, add focused regressions proving category-level drift changes the hash, tighten the checker/test harness, and validate with rch-only cargo-heavy commands. Acceptance criteria: 1. Changing category compliance totals/rates while preserving coarse counts changes content_hash. 2. Identical report inputs remain deterministic. 3. Checker/test coverage asserts the hash tracks category payload, not just counts. 4. Cargo-heavy validation uses rch and is reported in Agent Mail.","notes":"Patch landed on enterprise_governance hash surface. Local narrow validation passed: python checker + pytest + diff check + rustfmt file check. Remote rch cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets passed cleanly. Focused remote cargo test retries stalled/cancelled on pressure-affected workers (vmi1264463, vmi1227854 critical disk pressure), so authoritative Rust test execution remains infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"EmeraldCanyon","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:21:55.936422940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:44:55.215358457Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T14:44:55.215328712Z","close_reason":"Patch already landed and current-tree validation is sufficient: local checker PASS (21/21), pytest PASS (26 passed), rustfmt/diff clean, fresh rch package clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings passed on jain (exit 0, ~3m37s).
